briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 13, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Unprecedented Heatwaves and Intensification of Climate Disasters
sections.content: Globally, the convergence of 'Super El Niño' and climate change is leading to record-breaking heatwaves and abnormal high temperatures [3]. Domestically, Hayang-eup in Gyeongbuk recorded 39.9 degrees, prompting the first 'Severe Heatwave Warning' in Gyeongsan and Pohang. Tragically, heat-related illnesses increased fivefold in a single day, resulting in the death of an octogenarian [18, 21]. Europe is also suffering from relentless heatwaves, even at night [5], and the UK Met Office has stated that thousands of recent heatwave-related deaths were due to climate change [30]. In Spain, deadly wildfires have caused casualties [36], and warnings are growing louder that climate change is fundamentally altering our summers, intensifying disaster polarization, and claiming many victims [4, 27, 35].

sections.title: ⚠️ Widespread Climate Impacts on Ecosystems and the Economy
sections.content: Climate change is having widespread impacts not only on human lives but also across ecosystems and the economy. Domestically, concerns have been raised that changes in suitable aquaculture sites could provide grounds for restructuring the aquaculture industry [2], and in Gambia, saltwater intrusion has emerged as a frontline issue of climate change [22]. Furthermore, ecosystem disruption is accelerating due to climate change, with northern swallows becoming more vulnerable than southern swallows [24], and two giant Asian mantis species spreading across Europe [34]. Experts warn that climate change could also threaten the production of specific foods such as champagne, Comté cheese, and croissants [29], and could lead to hidden costs like soaring water bills [26, 28].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts and Cooperation for Climate Crisis Response
sections.content: Amidst the unprecedented climate crisis, various domestic and international efforts are continuing towards transitioning to a carbon-neutral society and responding to climate change. K-water and GGGI are strengthening global climate change response and water management cooperation [1], and Suwon City and Hwaseong Special Self-Governing City are spreading a culture of carbon neutrality practice through environmental education for infants and toddlers and the implementation of 'Zero Disposable Day' [8, 9]. UN EEPC is working to establish sustainable environmental governance through ESG practices from the sea to the Han River [11], and Boryeong City has laid the groundwork for promoting eco-friendly projects as its carbon neutrality and LNG cold energy projects passed the central investment review [10]. Furthermore, in response to heatwaves, Samsung and LG are pursuing strategies to 'cool down the heated Earth,' and Bucheon City is providing free bottled water to its citizens, demonstrating concrete adaptation efforts [7, 17].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Balancing Stable Energy Supply and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Amidst calls for nuclear power and renewable energy to work together for a stable electricity supply, which is key to national competitiveness [12], efforts towards energy transition are also continuing. Busan City has secured 20 billion won in national funding to establish a renewable energy production base in the Sinpyeong-Jangnim Industrial Complex [13], and arguments have been made that attracting high-tech industries is possible when creating AI and renewable energy logistics hubs [14]. Efforts to stabilize the power grid are also underway, including discussions on introducing ESS (Energy Storage Systems) to expand renewable energy connections in the Honam power grid [16]. However, with Minister Kim Sung-hwan of the Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment recently mentioning the need to consider additional nuclear power plant construction for the electricity supply of semiconductor and AI industrial complexes [20], finding a complex balance point in energy policy between surging electricity demand and achieving climate goals has emerged as a crucial challenge.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has clearly demonstrated the urgent threats and widespread impacts brought about by climate change, while also confirming that various domestic and international efforts are underway to address them. We must respond wisely to the climate crisis through continuous attention and cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.outlook: Through this initiative, it is expected to contribute to the prevention of heat-related illnesses among citizens during the heatwave period.

articles.description: Concerns are rising that the Ministry of Climate's renewable energy-centric transition policy is regressing, as Minister Kim Sung-hwan mentioned the need to consider additional nuclear power plant construction for semiconductor and AI industrial complex power supply.

summary.phenomenon: Minister Kim Sung-hwan of the Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment mentioned the necessity of reviewing the construction of additional nuclear power plants, citing the need for power supply to semiconductor and artificial intelligence (AI) industrial complexes.
summary.cause: The increasing electricity demand from semiconductor and AI industrial complexes is the main reason for the re-evaluation of energy policy.
summary.outlook: These remarks raise concerns that the Ministry of Climate's renewable energy-centric energy transition policy is regressing, and that the possibility of nuclear power plant expansion is being opened, moving from 'nuclear phase-out' to 'nuclear reduction' and now to 'nuclear expansion.'

articles.original_description: 재생에너지 후퇴 우려김성환 기후에너지환경부 장관이 반도체와 인공지능(AI) 산업단지 전력 공급을 이유로 추가 원자력발전소 건설 검토 필요성을 언급하면서, 기후부의 재생에너지 중심 에너지 전환 기조가 후퇴하는 것 아니냐는 우려가 나온다. ‘탈원전’ 인사로 분류됐던 김 장관은 취임 이후 ‘감원전’을 거쳐 최근에는 원전 증설 가능성까지 열어뒀다.김 장관은 지난 ···

articles.title: Hayan-eup Reaches 39.9°C…Gyeongsan and Pohang Issue First 'Extreme Heat Emergency Alert'
articles.original_title: 하양읍 최고 39.9도…경산·포항 첫 ‘폭염중대경보’ 발령
articles.description: The highest alert level, introduced this year, mandates adherence to 'survival guidelines,' including the suspension of outdoor activities. A double high-pressure system has led to the influx of hot and humid air, resulting in heatwave advisories across most of the country. On August 12th, Seoul's daytime temperature reached 35°C amidst scorching heat, with heatwave advisories in effect for most regions nationwide, including Gyeongsan City and Pohang in Gyeongsangbuk-do.

summary.phenomenon: On August 12th, heatwave advisories were issued across most regions of the country, with Hayang-eup in Gyeongsangbuk-do recording a high of 39.9°C and Seoul's daytime temperature rising to 35°C. Notably, Gyeongsan City and Pohang in Gyeongsangbuk-do issued the 'Extreme Heat Emergency Alert' for the first time, which is the highest alert level introduced this year.
summary.cause: This heatwave was caused by the influx of hot and humid air due to the influence of a double high-pressure system.
summary.outlook: Following the issuance of the Extreme Heat Emergency Alert, 'survival guidelines,' such as suspending outdoor activities, must be observed.

articles.original_title: UK couple found burned and semi-conscious in Almería amid Spanish wildfires
articles.description: Pair airlifted to hospital in two-hour rescue operation after Guardia Civil searched area for survivorsA British couple have been found badly burned and semi-conscious in a Spanish ravine amid deadly wildfires that have swept through the country’s Almería province, according to local media reports.The couple were on holiday in the region and were thought to be out hiking when they were caught up in the wildfire, which has so far killed 13 people and burned more than 6,000 hectares (14,800 acres)

summary.phenomenon: Deadly wildfires in Spain's Almería province have resulted in 13 deaths and burned over 6,000 hectares (14,800 acres). A British couple on holiday was found severely burned and semi-conscious due to these wildfires.

articles.title: Green MP Hannah Spencer to introduce bill on maximum workplace temperatures
articles.description: Byelection winner says heatwaves are causing ‘absolute chaos’ and workers need protection from unsafe conditionsThe Green MP Hannah Spencer is to introduce a bill in parliament that would pave the way for a maximum workplace temperature in the UK, as the country grapples with increasingly frequent heatwaves.If passed, the legislation will create an independent body to recommend maximum safe workplace temperatures and set out how those recommendations should be implemented. Continue reading...

summary.phenomenon: Increasingly frequent heatwaves in the UK are causing 'absolute chaos' in workplaces. (Specific figures or timelines are not mentioned in the article)
summary.outlook: Green MP Hannah Spencer plans to introduce a bill in the UK Parliament for a maximum workplace temperature. If passed, this legislation would establish an independent body to recommend maximum safe workplace temperatures and outline implementation strategies.
